blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I don't want to wait for him to make the next move. But I also don't want to go looking for a fight that isn't there yet.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I don't know. Be ready but not wound up. Stay visible but not exposed.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: That's actually the right answer bro.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Nah. But it is. Look — Daniel needs a moment. A reaction. A story he can tell about you. Right now you're giving him nothing to work with. Keep doing that.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: And what happens when nothing runs out?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Then you handle it. But you handle it on your terms, not his.
I thought about the side exit. The choice I made — whatever choice I made. How I'd felt standing there. And how I felt in the car afterward. Two very different things.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You ever get tired of being the smart one?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Dead serious, never. It's not exhausting. It's just — you see more. More things that could happen, more ways out, more of the play before it develops.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Doesn't that make it worse? Seeing all of it?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Only if you try to control all of it. If you just see it and then choose, it's actually easier.
On the field. In the car. On the steps. This kept showing up. See it. Choose. Go.
